meta.source: Cowork · FRED + TradingView Liquidity Monitor - Jae

liquidity.label: Tightening (repo stress persists)
decomp: No new H.4.1 print yet — TGA/Reserves unchanged

credit.label: Improving
decomp: HY OAS holding at 2.75% (no new print)
ALLY +0.28%, CACC +2.98%, COF +2.09% — lenders still firm
No credit stress despite the Korea/semis rout deepening

vol.label: Contained macro vol, Korea/semis crisis deepening
decomp: Gold +0.86% — first sign of a real safe-haven bid
KOSPI broke below 8,200 for the first time this cycle

bias: The Korea/semis rout deepened overnight rather than stabilizing: KOSPI -4.74% to 7,909.64 — now BELOW the 8,200 floor for the first time — with SK Hynix -8.05% and Samsung -6.60%. EWY -8.12% to $185.50. The Korea gate is now 0/3, down from 1/3 six hours ago and 2/3 yesterday morning: every condition is failing simultaneously. MU -10.57%, DRAM -10.82%, GLW -13.62%, LITE -6.63%, AMKR -7.24% all extended their declines. Broad macro measures are still notably calm — VIX +0.73%, MOVE actually -4.72%, and financials (ALLY/CACC/COF) are still firm with HY OAS holding at cycle tights — but gold is up +0.86%, the first real safe-haven signal in this episode. This pattern (contained VIX/MOVE/credit, but Asia equities and gold both moving) is consistent with a Korea/semiconductor-specific catalyst rather than a broad liquidity or rate event — check for chip-sector or Korea-specific news (export controls, a large guidance cut, tariff action) before treating this as resolved either way. Do not add to Korea or enabling-layer semis positions until the catalyst is identified and the gate reopens.

counterargs.thesis: Korea/semis rout is a specific catalyst, not a macro regime shift
supporting: VIX +0.73% — barely moved despite the scale of the Asia selloff
MOVE -4.72% — bond vol actually eased overnight
ALLY, CACC, COF all still firm; HY OAS holding at 2.75%
US10Y flat at 4.485% — no US rates contagion
against: KOSPI broke below 8,200 for the first time this cycle (-4.74%)
Korea gate fell to 0/3 — every condition now failing
SK Hynix -8.05%, Samsung -6.60% — the Korean chip majors, not just US-listed proxies
Gold +0.86% — the first genuine safe-haven bid in this episode
counterargs.read: Two sessions in, this still isn't showing up in US credit, US rates, or broad-market vol — which argues against a systemic liquidity event. But the fact that it's now hitting the underlying Korean chipmakers (not just US-listed proxies like MU/DRAM) and that gold is finally responding suggests this has legs and a real catalyst behind it, not just technical positioning unwinding. Until identified, treat the whole Korea/semis complex as untouchable — this is past the point of 'buy the dip' territory.

trade_table.rationale: Second straight session of losses: MU -10.57%, DRAM -10.82%, GLW -13.62%, SOXX -6.41%. This is no longer a one-day reversal — treat as thesis under review.
10Y essentially flat at 4.485%. No fresh signal either way; watch tomorrow's H.4.1 print for the next catalyst.
Gate fell to 0/3 — KOSPI broke its 8,200 floor overnight, EWY -8.12%, USDKRW still pinned above 1,530. All three conditions now failing simultaneously.
ALLY +0.28%, CACC +2.98%, COF +2.09% holding firm through two sessions of semis carnage. HY OAS steady at 2.75%. Genuine, persistent divergence.
DXY -0.07%, essentially flat overnight. USDJPY/USDKRW remain pinned at their thresholds without further deterioration.
HYG -0.48% but OAS unchanged at 2.75% — credit continues to not confirm the semis-equity stress, now into a second session.
Jul–Sep reserve drain still ahead, and this week is a live demonstration of how fast sector-specific shocks can compound for levered names.

catalysts.note: TGA + Reserves print due today, first new read since Jun 24. Will show if drain rate is accelerating.
Strong print = Warsh hike back on table. Weak print = duration relief.
Bid-to-cover and tail will show whether private demand absorbs supply at these higher yields.
PCE peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot CPI = duration selloff + Warsh hike.
APH $165/$170, DRAM $69/$74, EOSE $7.50/$10 spreads all expire — the Korea/semis rout has materially worsened the P&L picture here.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement. Auction sizes + TGA guidance for Q3.

chain.note: Second straight session of double-digit losses. $69/$74 Jul 17 spread (2 contracts) deep out of the money. Reassess urgently.
Now down ~20% combined over two sessions from the HBM-thesis highs. No confirmed catalyst yet.
Continuing to give back the earlier surge. Off $274 ATH. No active position.
Second session near -14%. 14 shares @ $189.66 — still above cost basis but the round-trip from the earlier spike is now complete. Investigate catalyst urgently.
Off $964 ATH, extending the pullback into a second session.
50 shares @ $38.57 — still above cost basis but the cushion is shrinking fast.
Off $96 ATH. Watchlist re-entry candidate, no active position.
$165/$170 Jul 17 spread still in the money but the margin is narrowing.
25 shares @ $84.21 — back below cost basis, unchanged from the prior read.
$7.50/$10 Jul 17 spread (10 contracts). Thesis broken, continuing to slide. CLOSE PRIORITY.

changed_today.delta: ↓ −10.82% — worst two-day stretch in the chain

changed_today.label: MOVE
changed_today.delta: ↓ −4.72% — bond vol easing despite the rout

vix.note: VIX essentially flat despite a second session of severe Korea/semis losses — reinforces the idiosyncratic-not-macro read, though it can't rule out a delayed reaction.

oil.note: Oil extends its decline, now well off the war-era highs. No inflation impulse from energy; supportive of the PCE-peak narrative.

gold.note: First genuine gold bid in this episode. Worth watching — if this persists alongside the Korea/semis rout, it argues for treating this as more than purely idiosyncratic.

move.note: Bond-market vol falling even as Asia equities crater. No signs of the stress migrating into rates markets yet.
